summary: Two ways to form future: perfective (present form = future) or imperfective (budem + infinitive)
Perfective future: just conjugate normally — prečítam = I will read (it through)
budem čítať = I will be reading (ongoing), prečítam = I will read (complete)
Negation: perfective → ne- on verb (neurobím), imperfective → ne- on auxiliary (nebudem robiť)
Reflexive perfective: naučím sa (I will learn), reflexive imperfective: budem sa učiť
Other irregular -ísť verbs follow same pattern: prídem, odídem, nájdem, vojdem, vyjdem
Modals use budem + infinitive: budem môcť, budem musieť, budem vedieť
Choose perfective for result/completion, imperfective for process/duration/habit

rules.title: The Two Future Tense Patterns
rules.text: Slovak future tense depends entirely on aspect. Perfective verbs already express future when conjugated in 'present' forms. Imperfective verbs need the auxiliary budem + infinitive.
examples: Prečítam knihu – I will read the book (done, perfective)
Budem čítať knihu – I will be reading the book (ongoing, imperfective)

rules.title: Future Auxiliary 'budem' — Conjugation
rules.text: Used only with imperfective verbs. Memorize these six forms.
examples: Budem pracovať zajtra – I will be working tomorrow
Budeš študovať? – Will you be studying?
Budú čakať celý deň – They will be waiting all day

rules.title: Perfective vs Imperfective Future — When to Use Which
rules.text: Same aspect logic as past tense: perfective = completed result, imperfective = ongoing/repeated process.

rows: budem písať – I will be writing
budem jesť – I will be eating
zjem – I will eat (all of it)
budem čítať – I will be reading
budem sa učiť – I will be studying
naučím sa – I will learn (master it)
budem variť – I will be cooking

examples: Budem čítať knihu celý deň – I'll be reading a book all day (process)
Prečítam knihu do zajtra – I'll finish reading the book by tomorrow (result)

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs in Future
rules.text: Perfective: conjugate + sa/si after verb. Imperfective: budem + sa/si + infinitive.

rows: naučím sa, umyjem sa, oblečiem sa
budem sa učiť, budem sa umývať

examples: Naučím sa slovenčinu – I will learn Slovak (perfective)
Budem sa učiť celý deň – I will be studying all day (imperfective)

rules.title: Negation
rules.text: Perfective: add ne- to the verb. Imperfective: add ne- to the auxiliary (nebudem). Simple and consistent.
examples: Neurobím to – I won't do it
Nebudem pracovať – I won't be working
Nenaučím sa to – I won't learn it

rules.title: Irregular Future: ísť and -ísť Verbs
rules.text: The verb ísť (to go) has a completely irregular future stem: pôjd-. All verbs ending in -ísť follow the same -íd- pattern.
examples: Pôjdem domov – I will go home
Prídeš na večeru? – Will you come to dinner?
Nájdem riešenie – I will find a solution
Nepôjdeme na prechádzku – We won't go for a walk

rules.title: Other Irregular Perfective Futures
rules.text: A few more common verbs with irregular future stems.
examples: Zjem obed – I will eat lunch
Vezmem si knihu – I will take a book
Vypiješ čaj? – Will you drink tea?

rules.title: Modals and State Verbs in Future
rules.text: Modal verbs and state verbs use budem + infinitive since they are imperfective (states, not completable actions).
examples: Budem môcť pomôcť? – Will I be able to help?
Nebudeme musieť čakať – We won't have to wait
Budeš vedieť slovenčinu – You will know Slovak
